headline: GST Was Killed. SST Doesn't Work. Now What?
context: Malaysia repealed the 6% GST in 2018 and reverted to SST, losing approximately RM20 billion in annual revenue and narrowing the tax base to 15% of the workforce.

cards.big: GST collected RM44B. SST collects RM28B. The gap is structural.
cards.sub: Only 15% of Malaysians pay income tax. The base is too narrow to fund the state.

cards.big: GST repeal was political. SST's inadequacy is mathematical.
cards.sub: SST cascades: tax on inputs taxed again on outputs. Consumers pay more, treasury gets less.
Without GST, Malaysia funds OECD-level ambitions on developing-nation revenue.

cards.big: E-invoicing mandate begins 2025 — laying GST infrastructure quietly.
cards.sub: LHDN's system mirrors GST compliance architecture. The path is being built.

cards.big: The question is not GST vs SST. It is who pays for the state.
cards.sub: A narrow tax base means either regressive consumption taxes or chronic deficits.

cards.big: Every tax system makes a choice about who contributes. Malaysia has not made that choice — it has avoided it.
